context.setting: Watching TV with TV in Front

episode_text:
day=4; meal=cantaloupes, bananas, watermelon; setting=Watching TV with TV in Front; time_of_day=morning;
preferences: microwave_time=no microwave; occlusion_relevance=minimize front occlusion; robot_speed=fast; skewering_axis=parallel to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=no; transfer_mode=outside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=near; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=LED; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=button; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=button; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=button; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=LED; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=button; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=button; retract_between_bites=yes; bite_dipping_preference=do not dip; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=10 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF food_condition is fruit or other cold-served food AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is any observed context, THEN no microwave BECAUSE the user explicitly chose no reheating for a fruit breakfast. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a warm savory meal, THEN 2 min BECAUSE the user explicitly chose a longer reheat time for chicken and hash brown than in the quieter routine setting. IF dining context involves working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a warm savory meal, THEN 2 min BECAUSE the user again selected a longer reheat time for a hot meal, suggesting a tendency toward hotter savory foods in attention-demanding contexts.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the user explicitly chose to preserve visibility toward the right-side TV. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the user again prioritized keeping the robot from blocking the right side, indicating sensitivity to right-side social/environmental engagement. IF dining context involves working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the user explicitly chose to keep the robot from blocking the front visual field while using the laptop. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the user selected front-occlusion minimization while watching a front-facing TV, reinforcing that visibility protection tracks the direction of attention.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a light cold fruit meal, THEN fast BECAUSE the user explicitly chose faster robot motion in that context, suggesting greater tolerance for speed when the meal is simple and attention is directed toward the TV. No broader context-dependent variation observed yet.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF feeding foods that require fork skewering AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any skewered food, THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE the user has limited mouth opening and repeatedly selected the narrower-bite option compatible with that constraint.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a skewered solid food meal, THEN yes BECAUSE the user explicitly chose confirmation in a social context, consistent with preferring extra verification when a failed bite transfer could be more awkward or noticeable. IF dining context involves working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a skewered solid food meal, THEN yes BECAUSE the user again chose explicit bite-acquisition confirmation, indicating a tendency toward extra verification in externally engaged contexts rather than only in quiet personal meals. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a fruit meal, THEN no BECAUSE the user skipped confirmation in that simpler meal, consistent with preferring efficiency when bite acquisition is likely straightforward and social awkwardness is low.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a routine meal AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user can lean forward comfortably and explicitly selected the less intrusive transfer style. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user maintained the same less intrusive transfer style in the social setting. IF dining context involves working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user again kept the less intrusive transfer style while engaged with a front-facing task. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user continued to prefer the less intrusive style in another front-facing attention context.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition in a routine meal, THEN near BECAUSE the user selected a close stopping distance while still preferring outside-mouth transfer. IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN medium BECAUSE the user chose a slightly larger buffer in the social setting, suggesting more interpersonal comfort distance there. IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND dining context involves working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN near BECAUSE the user returned to the closer stopping distance in the non-social but externally engaged context. IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN near BECAUSE the user again preferred the closer stopping distance in a front-facing non-social context.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a routine meal AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN speech BECAUSE the user explicitly preferred spoken readiness cues. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN LED BECAUSE the user switched to a quieter visual cue in the social setting, indicating sensitivity to conversational or social disruption. IF dining context involves working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN LED BECAUSE the user again preferred a visual cue in a context where audio may be more disruptive than helpful. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN LED BECAUSE the user selected a visual cue while attending to a front-facing TV, reinforcing a tendency to avoid spoken prompts when engaged with external media.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a routine meal AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN button BECAUSE the user has reliable arm control, can press buttons easily, and limited mouth opening makes open-mouth readiness less suitable. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN button BECAUSE the user again preferred explicit manual signaling, consistent with favoring reliable control over open-mouth detection in social interaction contexts. IF dining context involves working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN button BECAUSE the user maintained explicit manual readiness signaling even while multitasking, reinforcing a stable preference for reliable user-controlled initiation. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN button BECAUSE the user again chose manual readiness signaling, consistent with a strong overall preference for explicit control.

IF dining context is a routine meal AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN button BECAUSE the user has reliable arm control and explicitly preferred direct manual signaling. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN button BECAUSE the user maintained explicit manual signaling in the social setting. IF dining context involves working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN button BECAUSE the user again chose manual readiness signaling, indicating a stable preference for explicit control. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN button BECAUSE the user again selected manual readiness signaling in another externally engaged context.

IF dining context is a routine meal AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN button BECAUSE the user has reliable arm control and explicitly preferred direct manual signaling. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN button BECAUSE the user maintained explicit manual signaling in the social setting. IF dining context involves working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN button BECAUSE the user again chose manual readiness signaling, indicating a stable preference for explicit control. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN button BECAUSE the user again selected manual readiness signaling in another externally engaged context.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a routine meal AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN speech BECAUSE the user explicitly preferred spoken completion cues. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN LED BECAUSE the user switched to a visual completion cue in the social setting, consistent with preferring less disruptive signaling around another person. IF dining context involves working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN LED BECAUSE the user again preferred a visual completion cue while engaged with a front-facing task. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN LED BECAUSE the user selected a visual completion cue while watching TV, reinforcing a preference for quiet cues during externally focused activities.

detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF feeding task AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN perception BECAUSE the user consistently selected the reliable automatic completion method for bites across observed contexts.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF drinking task AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN button BECAUSE the user consistently preferred explicit manual completion signaling rather than automatic completion across observed contexts.

detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF mouth wiping task AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN button BECAUSE the user consistently preferred explicit manual completion signaling rather than automatic completion across observed contexts.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN yes BECAUSE the user chose to reduce prolonged visual obstruction in a context where right-side visibility matters. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN yes BECAUSE the user again chose retraction when right-side engagement mattered, indicating willingness to accept longer meal time to reduce obstruction in socially relevant contexts. IF dining context involves working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN yes BECAUSE the user also accepted retraction to reduce obstruction while maintaining visibility of a front-facing task. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN yes BECAUSE the user again chose retraction when front visibility mattered, suggesting a general tendency to retract when attending to media or tasks in the direction of gaze.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF food includes a dip or sauce AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is the observed TV-watching context, THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user explicitly chose to avoid dipping despite sauce being available. IF food_condition is a meal without sauces or dips AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is social with a person on the right, THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user selected no dipping in that meal as well. IF food includes a dip or sauce AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context involves working on a laptop in front, THEN less BECAUSE the user chose some dipping but limited the amount, suggesting a mess-averse compromise in a task-focused context. IF food_condition is fruit or another meal without dippable items or sauces AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context includes watching TV in front, THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user again selected no dipping in a meal where dipping was unnecessary.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a routine meal AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the user preferred a short timeout, consistent with favoring efficient progression when explicit button controls are available. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE the user chose a very long timeout in the social setting, indicating a strong preference to avoid unwanted automatic progression when attention may be shared with another person. IF dining context involves working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE the user again chose a very long timeout, indicating that when attention is divided by an external task they prefer to prevent automatic progression. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a light cold fruit meal, THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the user selected the short timeout in that context, suggesting that front-facing media alone does not always trigger a long wait when the meal is simple and the user still prefers efficient pacing.
